title: Best Portable Power Banks of 2026: Fast Charging Picks
excerpt: Never run out of battery again. We ranked the best power banks for phones, laptops, and everything in between.
author: CompareElite Team
intro:
Picking the wrong portable power bank means watching your phone drop to 0% two hours into a flight, waiting 4 hours for a slow trickle charge, or carrying a brick that weighs more than a book. The best portable power banks of 2026 have made serious leaps in fast charging output and capacity density — but a $22 model and an $89 model are not interchangeable, and choosing based on mAh alone will leave you frustrated. With dozens of options flooding Amazon, knowing which specs actually translate to real-world performance is the difference between staying connected and being stranded.

We tested more than 15 power banks across six criteria: total usable capacity versus rated capacity, maximum output wattage under sustained load, port configuration and cable compatibility, physical size and weight for daily carry, passthrough charging support, and safety certifications including UL and CE. Our lineup covers the Anker 737 Power Bank at the flagship end, the ultra-portable Anker Nano Power Bank 10K, the multi-device Baseus 65W Power Bank 20K, and the budget-friendly INIU Power Bank 22.5W — covering laptop users, light travelers, and cost-conscious buyers alike.

This guide includes a ranked comparison table for fast spec lookups, full individual reviews for each model, a buying guide covering the six factors that determine real-world performance, and a FAQ. Every power bank listed here is available on Amazon with current pricing and Prime-eligible fast shipping.

buying_guide.title: Total Capacity and Charging Cycles
buying_guide.body: Rated mAh capacity is measured at 3.7V internally, but your device charges at 5V — meaning actual usable capacity is typically 60-70% of the advertised number after voltage conversion losses. A 26,800mAh bank like the Anker 737 delivers roughly 16,000-18,000mAh of usable output — enough for 3-4 full iPhone charges. Most quality cells are rated for 500 charging cycles before capacity drops below 80%. The Anker 737 and Baseus 65W use high-grade lithium-ion cells tested to industry standards. Avoid no-name brands that overstate capacity with no third-party verification.

buying_guide.title: Fast Charging Output Wattage
buying_guide.body: Output wattage determines how quickly your device charges. Standard USB-A delivers 5W — too slow for most use cases. USB-C Power Delivery starts at 18W for phones and goes up to 140W for laptops. The Anker 737 Power Bank tops this guide at 140W USB-C output, enough to charge a MacBook Pro at near-wall speed. The Baseus 65W delivers 65W PD — sufficient for most ultrabooks and all smartphones. The Anker Nano Power Bank 10K outputs 30W, ideal for fast-charging phones. The INIU Power Bank delivers 22.5W — fast enough for phones but insufficient for laptops.

buying_guide.title: Number of Ports and Cable Types
buying_guide.body: Multiple ports let you charge several devices simultaneously, but total output wattage is shared across all active ports. Look for at least one high-wattage USB-C PD port plus a USB-A port for legacy cables. The Baseus 65W Power Bank 20K leads this guide with 4 ports — two USB-C and two USB-A — making it the best option for charging a laptop, phone, and earbuds simultaneously. The Anker 737 features two USB-C PD ports and one USB-A. The Anker Nano Power Bank 10K has just one port but includes a built-in USB-C cable, eliminating cable dependency for phone users.

buying_guide.title: Size and Weight for Travel
buying_guide.body: Power bank size is a direct tradeoff against capacity. Jacket-pocket portability requires under 200g and a slim form factor. The Anker Nano Power Bank 10K is the smallest in this guide at roughly credit-card thickness and under 180g — it disappears into any bag. The INIU Power Bank 22.5W is similarly slim at 10,000mAh. The Baseus 65W at 20,000mAh weighs around 450g — manageable in a backpack but too heavy for a pocket. The Anker 737 at 26,800mAh weighs 680g (1.5 lbs) and requires dedicated bag space. Match capacity to your actual carry habits, not your worst-case scenario.

buying_guide.title: Passthrough Charging Support
buying_guide.body: Passthrough charging lets you plug the power bank into a wall outlet while it simultaneously charges your connected devices. This is essential for desk setups and travel where outlet access is limited. The Anker 737 Power Bank supports passthrough charging at full output speed — plug into the wall and keep charging your laptop and phone without swapping cables. The Baseus 65W Power Bank 20K also supports passthrough. The Anker Nano Power Bank 10K and INIU Power Bank 22.5W have limited or no passthrough support. If you plan to use your power bank as a desktop charging hub, verify passthrough before buying.

buying_guide.title: Safety Certifications
buying_guide.body: Lithium-ion batteries carry genuine fire and explosion risks if built to poor standards. Look for UL certification (US safety standard), CE marking (EU), and built-in protections: overcharge protection, short-circuit protection, temperature control, and over-voltage cutoff. The Anker 737 carries UL certification and Anker's proprietary PowerIQ 4.0 safety technology, which monitors charging in real time. The Baseus 65W and INIU Power Bank both carry CE and RoHS certifications. Avoid power banks from unknown brands lacking any third-party certification — the cost savings are not worth the risk of a defective cell.

verdict: The Anker 737 Power Bank at $89 is the top pick in this guide for two reasons that no competitor at this size matches: 140W USB-C output that charges MacBook-class laptops at near-wall speed, and a 26,800mAh capacity with a smart display showing precise battery percentage. For budget buyers who need a daily phone companion, the INIU Power Bank 22.5W at $22 delivers genuine fast charging and a slim design for less than a quarter of the flagship price. Both picks and the full lineup are available on Amazon with Prime shipping — current prices are listed in this guide and reflect real-time Amazon availability.

faq.q: Can a power bank charge a laptop?
faq.a: Yes, but only if it outputs 45W or more via USB-C PD. The Anker 737 (140W) and Baseus 65W are great laptop-compatible options.

faq.q: Are power banks allowed on airplanes?
faq.a: Yes, in carry-on bags only. Most airlines allow power banks up to 100Wh (about 27,000mAh at 3.7V). Check your airline's specific policy.
